title: Redact secrets in formatted output
scenario: Logging helpers should make accidental secret exposure difficult. A wrapper can intentionally hide sensitive values when formatted.
instructions: Keep the existing log event types. Define Secret and implement Display so formatting always writes [redacted].

hints.body: Add a Secret(String) wrapper with new and expose methods; keep LogLevel, LogFields, and LogEvent in the file.
Implement std::fmt::Display for Secret and write the literal [redacted], not the inner string.
One complete solution for Redact secrets in formatted output is shown below.

completionExplanation: Secret now gives callers an explicit wrapper whose formatted output is redacted while the previous structured event types remain available.
